exploration_log.aha_moment: lexical_cast succeeds for 'nan' and 'inf' because the underlying std::stof/stod does accept those strings; so successful conversion doesn't mean the value is usable in a range. Need an explicit isfinite check gated on std::is_floating_point.
exploration_log.hindsight: None — the fix was exactly in the right place.

alternatives: Cast to long double for isfinite
key_decisions.decision: Cast to double for std::isfinite check
key_decisions.reasoning: double covers all non-long-double floating-point types; for long double types isfinite(val) can be called directly, but the cast to double is safe for detecting nan/inf in all practical cases

queries.query_text: Add isfinite check to Range validator in Validators.hpp to reject NaN and inf floating-point values

solve_notes: Added an explicit `std::isfinite` check inside the Range validator's lambda, gated on `std::is_floating_point<T>::value`, to reject NaN and infinity before the range bounds check. The cast to `double` for `isfinite` ensures it works for all floating-point specializations.
task_text:
The `Range` validator in `Validators.hpp` uses `lexical_cast` for
conversion but does not handle special floating-point values (`NaN`,
`inf`, `-inf`). When a user passes `nan` to a `Range`-validated
float option, the behavior is undefined. Add explicit rejection of
non-finite values in the `Range` validator with a clear error message.
